description_long: This bungalow features a long, rectangular footprint with a prominent gable roof covered in dark gray shingles. The front facade is defined by a wide, covered porch with railings and steps leading up to the main entrance, flanked by multiple windows. The exterior walls are clad in light-colored horizontal siding, and the structure is elevated on a visible foundation. The rear side includes a secondary entrance with steps and a small deck, while the sides display a series of evenly spaced windows. The overall design is simple yet functional, with architectural details such as exposed rafters and decorative trim. The bungalow is well-suited for residential neighborhoods and suburban settings.
description: A long, single-story bungalow with a gable roof, wide front porch, and light horizontal siding. Features include multiple windows, a rear deck, and decorative trim.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wide covered porch, main entrance, and three large front windows.
description_view_1: Side view displaying a row of evenly spaced windows and the length of the gable roof.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the secondary entrance, small deck, and minimal window arrangement.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with a visible side door, steps, and additional windows along the facade.
attribute_window_arrangement: symmetrical front-facing windows with evenly spaced side windows
